reasoning: The document shows excellent content preservation with a composite score of 0.9727 and near-perfect programmatic shingling metrics. However, there are two major structural defects: a continuous Roman numeral list is broken across pages, and one bare URL was not converted to a functional hyperlink. Per the count-based routing thresholds, the presence of these 2 major defects requires HUMAN_REVIEW for structural correction.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a continuous numbered list using Roman numerals (I through X) spanning all three pages. However, the output breaks this list. While items I-IV on page 1 and VII-X on page 3 are converted into list arrays, items V and VI on page 2 are converted into a heading and a standard paragraph, respectively, completely losing their list structure. This is a defect where numbered items are rendered as plain text/headings instead of list items.
